name: Primordial black hole abundance from microlensing surveys
requirement: Any candidate framework predicting primordial black holes (PBHs) as a significant DM component must respect microlensing constraints from MACHO, EROS, OGLE, and HSC Subaru surveys. PBHs constitute < ~10% of dark matter for masses 10^-11 - 10 M_sun; tighter limits in some sub-windows. Larger masses constrained by CMB / dwarf-galaxy heating; smaller masses by Hawking-evaporation gamma-ray bounds.

regime.description: Asteroid-to-stellar mass scales for compact objects in the galactic halo and Milky Way - M31 light path.

current_bound.value: f_PBH < 0.1 for 10^-11 < M_PBH/M_sun < 10; tighter in specific sub-windows
current_bound.experiment: OGLE + Subaru HSC microlensing toward M31
current_bound.reference: Niikura et al., 'Microlensing constraints on primordial black holes with Subaru/HSC Andromeda observations', Nature Astron. 3, 524 (2019), arXiv:1701.02151; Carr et al., Rep. Prog. Phys. 84, 116902 (2021), arXiv:2002.12778 (review)

what_it_excludes: Severely constrains PBH DM scenarios. Open windows for PBHs as 100% of DM remain narrow: the 'asteroid-mass window' 10^-17 - 10^-12 M_sun (between Hawking-evaporation and microlensing) and possibly the Stupar-mass window. Solar-mass PBHs as DM are essentially excluded.

parameterization.name: PBH mass function (monochromatic or extended) plus DM fraction f_PBH = Omega_PBH / Omega_DM.
parameterization.reference: Carr, Kuhnel, Sandstad, PRD 94, 083504 (2016), arXiv:1607.06077; Carr et al., Rep. Prog. Phys. 84, 116902 (2021).
parameterization.summary: Different observables constrain different mass ranges: Hawking gamma-rays (M < 10^17 g), microlensing (10^-11 - 10 M_sun), CMB accretion (M > 10 M_sun), GW dynamical effects in clusters.

procedural.predicted_observable: Number of microlensing events as a function of duration in surveys with known optical-depth maps.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: f_PBH(M) bounded as a function of mass; full constraint plot in Carr et al. 2021.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts PBHs (e.g. from inflationary spike, phase transitions, scalar collapse), compute the mass function dN/dM and the total DM fraction.

primary_references.citation: Niikura et al., 'Microlensing constraints on primordial black holes with Subaru/HSC Andromeda observations', Nature Astron. 3, 524 (2019)
Carr, Kohri, Sendouda, Yokoyama, 'Constraints on primordial black holes', Rep. Prog. Phys. 84, 116902 (2021)
